completed: Preview chapter payloads now expose source counts without exposing the underlying citation library.
SourcesPage now shows anonymous readers a gated chapter-by-chapter source summary and signed-in readers the full bibliography.
SearchPage now uses a server-backed /api/search endpoint that returns preview-safe results anonymously and full source-text matches after sign-in.

Signed-in readers can now filter search results by source-only matches, evidence tier, and chapter format, while the Sources page supports hierarchy and evidence-mix filtering without widening anonymous payloads.
Auth modal flows now preserve locked search and source-browser intent across signup or login, and explicit log-in actions now open in login mode instead of the default signup state.
Verification completed with npx tsc --noEmit, npm run build, local API checks, and live anonymous/authenticated search validation.
next_task: Reduce jspdf and vendor-react bundle weight, then continue the authenticated archive experience with richer source-browser filters while Brandon handles portal-side growth setup.
next_priorities: Reduce jspdf and vendor-react bundle weight.
Add institution and source-type filters plus filtered bibliography export/share views.
Connect HubSpot workflows, double opt-in, and lifecycle scoring in the portal.
Connect Google Search Console and verify production funnel events in GA4 plus HubSpot.
